name: Mexico
summary: Mexico was the first sovereign country to use catastrophe bonds as part of its disaster risk financing framework. Through FONDEN and later World Bank-supported transactions, Mexico transferred earthquake and hurricane risk to capital markets.
policyRelevance: Mexico is the most important benchmark sovereign case for catastrophe bonds. It demonstrates repeated sovereign issuance, multi-peril structuring, and integration of cat bonds into a broader public disaster risk financing system.
modelType: Sovereign Cat Bond (FONDEN / World Bank CAR Notes)

presentations.title: Mexico Catastrophe Bond Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on Mexico's sovereign catastrophe bond framework.

pdfs.title: FONDEN Mexico Cat Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: World Bank case study on Mexico's catastrophe bond program.

pdfs.title: Mexico MultiCat Program
pdfs.description: World Bank document on the MultiCat platform used by Mexico.

pdfs.title: Mexico 2024 Cat Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: Recent case study on Mexico's renewed catastrophe bond transaction.

notes: First sovereign catastrophe bond issuer globally.
Repeated sovereign market access makes Mexico a flagship case.
Highly relevant for sovereign fiscal resilience analysis.

name: Chile
summary: Chile entered the sovereign catastrophe bond market through the Pacific Alliance transaction and later returned with a standalone World Bank-supported catastrophe bond and swap structure focused on earthquake risk.
policyRelevance: Chile is highly relevant as a sovereign with major seismic risk and a disciplined fiscal-policy framework. It is one of the strongest comparators for sovereign earthquake-focused risk transfer.
modelType: Sovereign Cat Bond via World Bank

presentations.title: Chile Catastrophe Bond Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on Chile's sovereign catastrophe bond case.

pdfs.title: Pacific Alliance Catastrophe Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: World Bank case study covering Chile, Colombia, Peru, and Mexico in the Pacific Alliance structure.

pdfs.title: Chile 2023 Cat Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: World Bank case study on Chile's 2023 sovereign catastrophe bond transaction.

notes: Important sovereign earthquake-focused case.
Combines strong public finance logic with capital markets risk transfer.
Useful comparator for fiscally disciplined sovereigns.

name: Colombia
heroTitle: Colombia in the Pacific Alliance Catastrophe Bond
summary: Colombia participated in the Pacific Alliance catastrophe bond, a regional sovereign transaction supported by the World Bank to provide earthquake protection through capital markets.
policyRelevance: Colombia is a strong example of multi-country sovereign risk transfer. It shows how regional structures can reduce costs and support capital markets access for disaster risk financing.

presentations.title: Colombia Catastrophe Bond Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on Colombia's participation in the Pacific Alliance cat bond.

pdfs.title: Pacific Alliance Catastrophe Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: World Bank case study covering the regional sovereign issuance.

notes: Regional sovereign structure.
Illustrates pooled or coordinated sovereign issuance logic.
Useful case for multi-country policy design.

name: Peru
heroTitle: Peru in the Pacific Alliance Catastrophe Bond
summary: Peru obtained sovereign earthquake protection through the Pacific Alliance catastrophe bond structure supported by the World Bank.
policyRelevance: Peru is relevant as part of a sovereign disaster risk financing framework that combines market instruments with other public financial protection tools.

presentations.title: Peru Catastrophe Bond Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on Peru's participation in the Pacific Alliance cat bond.

pdfs.title: Pacific Alliance Catastrophe Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: World Bank case study covering Peru's earthquake protection structure.

notes: Part of multi-country sovereign issuance.
Relevant for layered sovereign disaster risk financing.
Useful for comparing regional transaction design.

name: Philippines
summary: The Philippines issued the first sovereign catastrophe bond in Asia, transferring typhoon and earthquake risk to capital markets with World Bank support.
policyRelevance: The Philippines is an especially important case because it combines multiple disaster perils in a sovereign emerging-market setting and demonstrates the role of cat bonds in rapid post-disaster liquidity.
modelType: Sovereign Cat Bond via World Bank

presentations.title: Philippines Catastrophe Bond Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on the Philippines sovereign cat bond transaction.

pdfs.title: Philippines Catastrophe Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: Case study and supporting materials on the Philippines sovereign risk transfer transaction.

pdfs.title: OECD Catastrophe Bond Markets in Asia-Pacific
pdfs.description: OECD report covering catastrophe bond development in Asia and including the Philippines case.

notes: First sovereign cat bond in Asia.
Multi-peril sovereign transaction.
Strong example for emerging-market fiscal resilience.

name: Jamaica
summary: Jamaica became the first small island developing state to independently sponsor a catastrophe bond, using the capital markets to secure protection against named storms and hurricanes.
policyRelevance: Jamaica is highly relevant for climate-vulnerable sovereigns, especially where rapid post-disaster liquidity and donor-supported transaction design are important.
modelType: Sovereign Cat Bond via World Bank

presentations.title: Jamaica Catastrophe Bond Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on Jamaica's sovereign catastrophe bond transaction.

pdfs.title: Jamaica Catastrophe Bond Case Study
pdfs.description: World Bank case study on Jamaica's catastrophe bond transaction.

pdfs.title: Jamaica Catastrophe Bond Additional Case Study
pdfs.description: Additional case study and supporting material related to Jamaica's cat bond.

notes: First small island state to independently sponsor a cat bond.
Strong climate resilience case.
Important comparator for disaster-vulnerable sovereigns.

name: Israel
heroTitle: Israel - Disaster Risk, Insurance Market, and Catastrophe Bond Relevance
summary: Israel has not issued a sovereign catastrophe bond. However, it is highly relevant from a policy perspective due to its earthquake exposure and the potential future role of catastrophe bonds in sovereign disaster risk financing.
policyRelevance: Israel should be presented as a policy relevance case rather than a sovereign issuance case. The focus should be on earthquake risk, public financial protection, insurance market structure, and the possible future use of catastrophe bonds.
modelType: Potential Future Sovereign Case / Policy Relevance

presentations.title: Israel and Catastrophe Bonds - Policy Presentation
presentations.description: Presentation on Israel's disaster risk profile and the possible relevance of catastrophe bonds.

pdfs.title: OECD Review of Disaster Risk Management in Israel
pdfs.description: OECD report on disaster risk management and resilience in Israel.

notes: No sovereign catastrophe bond issuance to date.
Should be framed as a future policy-use case.
Focus on earthquake risk and sovereign disaster risk financing implications.
